source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two non-relativistic particles of equal energy and equal mass collide almost head-on. In a coordinate frame (the center of mass frame) moving with velocity $\mathbf{V}$, the particles appear to collide head-on. Compare the total energy in the center of mass system to the original total energy.

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Two non-relativistic particles of equal energy and equal mass collide almost head-on. In a coordinate frame (the center of mass frame) moving with velocity $\mathbf{V}$, the particles appear to collide head-on. Compare the total energy in the center of mass system to the original total energy.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image fields contain PIL object representations rather than file paths or URLs
force_requires_image inferred from record metadata `vision_relevance: essential` and image caption
metadata.reasoning_chain: In the center of mass frame, the velocity of $m_{1}$ is computed as $\mathbf{V}_{1}'=\dot{\mathbf{r}}_{1}-\dot{\mathbf{r}}_{c}=u\mathbf{i}-\frac{u}{2}\left[(1-\cos\theta_{0})\mathbf{i}-\sin\theta_{0}\mathbf{j}\right]=\frac{u}{2}\left[(1+\cos\theta_{0})\mathbf{i}+\sin\theta_{0}\mathbf{j}\right]$, and the velocity of $m_{2}$ is $\mathbf{V}_{2}'=\dot{\mathbf{r}}_{2}-\dot{\mathbf{r}}_{c}=-u(\cos\theta_{0}\mathbf{i}+\sin\theta_{0}\mathbf{j})-\frac{u}{2}\left[(1-\cos\theta_{0})\mathbf{i}-\sin\theta_{0}\mathbf{j}\right]=-\frac{u}{2}\left[(1+\cos\theta_{0})\mathbf{i}+\sin\theta_{0}\mathbf{j}\right]$. The total energy in the center of mass frame is then $E'=\frac{1}{2}m\left(V_{1}'^{2}+V_{2}'^{2}\right)=\frac{1}{2}mu^{2}(1+\cos\theta_{0})$. Since the original total energy is $E=mu^{2}$, the ratio of the energies is $\frac{E'}{E}=\frac{1+\cos\theta_{0}}{2}$.

problem_main_record.reasoning_chain: In the center of mass frame, the velocity of $m_{1}$ is computed as $\mathbf{V}_{1}'=\dot{\mathbf{r}}_{1}-\dot{\mathbf{r}}_{c}=u\mathbf{i}-\frac{u}{2}\left[(1-\cos\theta_{0})\mathbf{i}-\sin\theta_{0}\mathbf{j}\right]=\frac{u}{2}\left[(1+\cos\theta_{0})\mathbf{i}+\sin\theta_{0}\mathbf{j}\right]$, and the velocity of $m_{2}$ is $\mathbf{V}_{2}'=\dot{\mathbf{r}}_{2}-\dot{\mathbf{r}}_{c}=-u(\cos\theta_{0}\mathbf{i}+\sin\theta_{0}\mathbf{j})-\frac{u}{2}\left[(1-\cos\theta_{0})\mathbf{i}-\sin\theta_{0}\mathbf{j}\right]=-\frac{u}{2}\left[(1+\cos\theta_{0})\mathbf{i}+\sin\theta_{0}\mathbf{j}\right]$. The total energy in the center of mass frame is then $E'=\frac{1}{2}m\left(V_{1}'^{2}+V_{2}'^{2}\right)=\frac{1}{2}mu^{2}(1+\cos\theta_{0})$. Since the original total energy is $E=mu^{2}$, the ratio of the energies is $\frac{E'}{E}=\frac{1+\cos\theta_{0}}{2}$.
